assertions.title: Librarian can navigate to librarian routes without auth errors

assertions.issues: Browser automation tool (agent-browser) failed to start daemon - could not test full authentication flow

blockers.description: agent-browser tool failed to start daemon with persistent error: 'Daemon failed to start (port: 127.0.0.1:50838)'. Multiple attempts were made including: killing existing processes, cleaning lock files, using different session names, setting environment variables, and waiting extended periods. The daemon could not initialize despite the tool being installed and Chromium being available.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: 1. agent-browser close (successful); 2. Removed stale .pid and .port files from ~/.agent-browser; 3. Killed chrome.exe processes; 4. Waited 10+ seconds between attempts; 5. Tried --native flag; 6. Tried fresh PowerShell session; 7. Tried setting AGENT_BROWSER_HOME to temp directory; 8. Verified no port conflicts with netstat. All attempts failed with same daemon error.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 4 blocked (browser automation tool daemon failed to start). Code analysis confirms all route configurations are correct, components exist, and HTTP responses are 200. Unable to perform full UI testing including login flow and visual verification due to agent-browser daemon startup failure.
